question: An empty tank has concave mirror as its bottom. When sunlight falls normally on the mirror, it is focused at a height of 32$cm$ from the mirror. If the tank is filled with water up to a height of 20$cm$ , then the sunlight focuses at (refractive index of water $=\dfrac{4}{3}$)
explanation:
Given, Image distance, $v=-32 {~cm}$
Object (sun) distance, $u=\infty$
Tank is filled upto $=20 {~cm}$
Refractive index of water $=\dfrac{4}{3}$
Using mirror formula,
$\dfrac{1}{f}=\dfrac{1}{v}+\dfrac{1}{u} \Rightarrow \dfrac{1}{f}=\dfrac{-1}{32}+\dfrac{1}{\infty}$
$f=-32 {~cm}$
When water is filled upto a height $h=20 {~cm}$, the image formed by mirror will acts as virtual object for water surface.
The image formed by virtual object. https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764958179/preptracker/questions/q_112810_explanation_1764958178916.png $\dfrac{n_{\text {water }}}{n_{\text {air }}}=\dfrac{\text { Actual height }}{\text { Apparent height }}$
$\dfrac{4}{3}=\dfrac{X O}{X I}[X O=12 {~cm}]$
$\dfrac{4}{3}=\dfrac{12}{X I} \Rightarrow X I=9 {~cm}$
Sunlight will focus at $9{\text{ }}cm$ above water level. So, the correct option is (2)
subject: Physics
subtopic: Miscellaneous Problems on Refraction at Plane Surface

question: A light ray travels from a medium with refractive index $n_{1}$ to another medium of refractive index $n_{2}$. If $n_{1}=2$ and $n_{2}=\sqrt{3}$, then find the critical angle.
explanation:
Given, $n_{1}=2, n_{2}=\sqrt{3}$
If $i_{c}$ be the critical angle, then
$\sin i_{c}=\dfrac{1}{{ }_{2} n_{1}}=\dfrac{1}{\dfrac{n_{1}}{n_{2}}}=\dfrac{n_{2}}{n_{1}}=\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$
$\Rightarrow \sin i_{c}=\sin 60^{\circ}$
$\Rightarrow i_{c}=60^{\circ}$
So, the correct option is (4)
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: Figure shows a ray of light entering and passing through a dense glass slab and emerging from the other side. If the angle $i=60^{\circ}$, slab thickness $b=0.04 {~m}$ and the refractive index of glass $=\sqrt{3}$, the parallel Shift $-d$ between the emerging and entering rays in $m m$ is
explanation:
Ray diagram is shown below. https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764952872/preptracker/questions/q_112775_explanation_1764952872318.png We have, $\sin r=\dfrac{\sin i}{n}=\dfrac{\sin 60^{\circ}}{\sqrt{3}}=\dfrac{1}{2}$
$\Rightarrow$ Angle of refraction, $r=30^{\circ}$
Now, lateral Shift $d$ is given by
$d=b \cdot \sec (r) \cdot \sin (i-r)$
$=0.04 \times \sec \left(30^{\circ}\right) \cdot \sin \left(60^{\circ}-30^{\circ}\right) {m}$
$=0.04 \times \tan 30^{\circ} {m}$
$=\dfrac{0.04}{\sqrt{3}} m=\dfrac{0.04 \times 1000}{\sqrt{3}} m m=\dfrac{40}{\sqrt{3}} m m$
So, the correct option is (3)
subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question: A printed page is kept pressed by a transparent cube of edge $t$. The refractive index of the cube varies as $n(z)=1+\dfrac{z}{t}$, where $z$ is the vertical distance from bottom of the cube. If viewed from top, then the printed letters appear to be shifted by an amount.
explanation:
The arrangement is shown below https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764958162/preptracker/questions/q_112769_explanation_1764958162374.png Consider an elemental strip at a height $z$ of thickness $d z$.
Apparent height $=\dfrac{\text { Real height }}{R I}=\dfrac{d z}{n(z)}$
$d h=\dfrac{d z}{1+\dfrac{z}{t}}=\left(\dfrac{t}{t+z}\right) d z$
$h=\int_{0}^{t} \dfrac{t}{t+z} d z$
$h=[t \ln (t+z)]_{0}^{t}=t[\ln (t+t)-\ln (t+0)]$
$h=t \ln \dfrac{2 t}{t}=t \ln 2$
Shift, $\Delta x=t-t \ln 2$
$\Delta x=(1-\ln 2) t$
So, the correct option is (1)
subject: Physics

question: Critical angle of incidence for a pair of optical media is $45^{\circ}$. The refractive indices of first and second media are in the ratio
explanation:
$\sin c=\dfrac{n_{2}}{n_{1}} \Rightarrow \dfrac{n_{2}}{n_{1}}=\sin 45^{\circ}=\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$
$\Rightarrow \dfrac{n_{1}}{n_{2}}=\sqrt{2}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: A converging beam is incident on a system of two glass slabs with refractive indices ${\mu_{1}=(3 / 2), \mu_{2}=(5 / 4)}$ and a concave mirror placed at a distance ' ${l}$ ' from the closest face of the slab 2 . The observer ${O}$ receives the reflected rays from the system and perceives that the image is formed at ${P}$ itself (see figure) which is also the point of convergence of the incident beam (extrapolated). If the concave mirror's focal length is 20 $cm$ . Find the value of ${l}$
explanation:
An image is formed at position $P$, it means point $P$ should be the centre of curvature of the mirror.
Shift due to slabs $S=S_{1}+S_{2}$
$=6\left(1-\dfrac{2}{3}\right)+5\left(1-\dfrac{4}{5}\right)=3 {~cm}$
The shift should be in the direction of ray travelling, hence the ray should conserve $17+3=20 {~cm}$ from the right face of the slab (2).
Hence $l=17+3+2 \times 20=60 {~cm}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Miscellaneous Problems on Refraction at Plane Surface

question: An opaque cylindrical tank with an open top has a diameter of 3.00 $m$ and is filled with water. When the setting sun reaches an angle of ${37^{\circ}}$ above the horizon, sunlight ceases to illuminate any part of the bottom of the tank. How deep is the tank  ?
explanation:
By Snell's law, $1 \sin 53^{\circ}=\dfrac{4}{3} \sin r$, https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764955664/preptracker/questions/q_33642_explanation_1764955663523.png $\dfrac{4}{5}=\dfrac{4}{3} \sin r, \sin r=\dfrac{3}{5} \Rightarrow r=37^{\circ}$
In $\Delta OAB,\tan \,\,r = 3/H = \tan 37^\circ \Rightarrow H = 4\,m$
subject: Physics

question: A rod made of glass ${(\mu=1.5)}$ and of square cross-section is bent into the shape shown in figure. A parallel beam of light falls perpendicularly on the plane flat surface ${d}$. Referring to the diagram, ${d}$ is the width of a side and ${R}$ the radius of inner semi-circle. Find the maximum value of ratio ${(d / R)}$ so that all light entering the glass through surface ${A}$ emerge from the glass through ${B}$.
explanation:
In order that all the light rays entering at end ${A}$ emerge out from end ${B}$, the minimum angle of incident ${i}$ for the light ray (say ${P Q}$ as shown in figure), should be equal to or greater than critical angle ${\theta_{c}}$ for air glass interface. If it happens for ray ${P Q}$ then for all the light rays total internal reflection occurs at inner surface of rod and all these rays will emerge out only from end ${B}$. https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764951144/preptracker/questions/q_33630_explanation_1764951143690.png Thus, here we use ${i \geq \theta_{c}=\sin ^{-1}\left(\dfrac{1}{\mu}\right) \Rightarrow \sin i \geq \dfrac{1}{\mu}}$
From figure, we have ${\sin i=\dfrac{P O}{O Q}=\dfrac{R}{(R+d)}}$
${\Rightarrow \dfrac{R}{(R+d)} \geq \dfrac{1}{1.5} \Rightarrow 1.5 R \geq(R+d)}$
${\Rightarrow 0.5 R \geq d}$ or ${d / R \leq 0.5 \Rightarrow\left(\dfrac{d}{R}\right)_{\max }=0.5}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: A rod of height $h$ is placed in a beaker of same radius. The height of the beaker is thrice its radius. An observer sees the top end of the rod through a pin-hole (see figure).  When the beaker is filled with a liquid  $\left( {R.I. = \sqrt \mu  } \right)$ upto a height 2h, he can see the lower end of the rod. Find the value of  $\mu $.
explanation:
The line of sight of the observer remains constant, making an angle of $45^\circ $ with the normal.
$\sin \theta=\dfrac{{h}}{\sqrt{{h}^{2}+(2 {~h})^{2}}}$$=\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{5}}$
Refractive index of liquid, https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764955653/preptracker/questions/q_33470_explanation_1764955652525.png $\sqrt \mu {\rm{ }} = \frac{{\sin 45^\circ }}{{\sin \theta }}$
$ = \frac{{\frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}}}{{\frac{1}{{\sqrt 5 }}}} = \sqrt {\left( {\frac{5}{2}} \right)} $
$\therefore \sqrt{\mu}=\sqrt{2.5} \quad \Rightarrow \mu=2.5$
subject: Physics

question: A light wave has a frequency of ${4 \times 10^{14} {~Hz}}$ and a wavelength of ${5 \times 10^{-7} {~m}}$ in a medium. The refractive index of the medium is
explanation:
Speed of light in medium ${=v \lambda}$
${=4 \times 10^{14} \times 5 \times 10^{-7}=20 \times 10^{7} {~m} {~s}^{-1}}$
$\mu = \frac{{{\text{ Speed of light in vacuum }}}}{{{\text{ Speed of light in medium }}}} = \frac{{3 \times {{10}^8}}}{{20 \times {{10}^7}}}$
${\text{ = 1}}{\text{.5}}$.
So correct option is (1)
subject: Physics

question: Light of wavelength 5000$ \mathop A^{~~\circ}  $ in air has wavelength in glass ${(\mu=1.5)}$ equal to
explanation:
Given, ${\lambda _1} = 5000$$ \mathop A^{~~\circ} $ ${\mu _1}({\text{air}}) = 1,{\mu _2}({\text{glass}}) = 1.5,$
${\lambda _2} = ? $
$\frac{{{\mu _2}}}{{{\mu _1}}} = \frac{{{\lambda _1}}}{{{\lambda _2}}} \Rightarrow {\lambda _2} = \frac{{{\lambda _1}{\mu _1}}}{{{\mu _2}}} = \frac{{5000}}{{1.5}} \approx 3333$$ \mathop A^{~~\circ} $.
So correct option is (3)
subject: Physics

question: A fish, looking up through the water, sees the outside world contained in a circular horizon. If the refractive index of water is $4 / 3$ and the fish is$12\,cm$ below the surface of water, the radius of the circle in centimeter is
explanation:
Given situation is shown in the figure.
Here $h = 12\,cm$
$\begin{aligned}& \mu=\dfrac{4}{3}
\\& \tan \theta_{c}=\dfrac{r}{h}
\\& r=h \tan \theta_{c}
\\& r=h \dfrac{\sin \theta_{c}}{\cos \theta_{c}}\end{aligned}$ https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764955651/preptracker/questions/q_33387_explanation_1764955651289.png $r=h \dfrac{\sin \theta_{c}}{\sqrt{1-\sin ^{2} \theta_{c}}}$
Here, $\sin \theta_{c}=\dfrac{1}{\mu}$
$\therefore r=h \dfrac{\dfrac{1}{\mu}}{\sqrt{1-\dfrac{1}{\mu^{2}}}}=\dfrac{h}{\sqrt{\mu^{2}-1}}=\dfrac{12}{\sqrt{\dfrac{16}{9}-1}}$
$\,\,\,\,\,\,$$ = \frac{{12 \times 3}}{{\sqrt 7 }}\,cm$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: A layer of benzene $(\mu  = 1.5)6\,cm$ deep floats on water$\left( {\mu  = \frac{4}{3}} \right),4\,cm$ deep. When viewed vertically through air, the apparent distance of the bottom of the vessel below the free surface of benzene is
explanation:
Apparent depth $=\dfrac{t_{b}}{\mu_{b}}+\dfrac{t_{w}}{\mu_{w}}$
$ = \frac{{6\,cm}}{{1.5}} + \frac{{4\,cm}}{{4/3}} = (4 + 3)cm = 7\,cm$.
subject: Physics

question: A vessel is half filled with a liquid of refractive index $\mu$. The other half of the vessel is filled with an immiscible liquid of refractive index $1.5 \mu$. The apparent depth of the vessel is $50 \%$ of the actual depth. Then $\mu$ is
explanation:
Let $d$ be the depth of two liquids. Then apparant depth
$\frac{{(d/2)}}{\mu } + \frac{{(d/2)}}{{1.5\mu }} = \frac{d}{2}$ or $\frac{1}{\mu } + \frac{2}{{3\mu }} = 1$
Solving we get $\mu=1.671$
subject: Physics

question: A fish looking up through the water sees the outside world contained in a circular horizon. If the redfractive index of water is $4 / 3$ and the fish is $12\,cm$. below the surface, the radius of this circle in $cm$ is
explanation:
$\sin \,\,{i_C} = \frac{1}{\mu } = \frac{r}{{\sqrt {{r^2} + {h^2}} }}$
https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764955646/preptracker/questions/q_33355_explanation_1764955645497.png
Using $h = 12\,cm,\,\,\,\mu = 4/3$
We get $\frac{{36}}{{\sqrt 7 }}\,cm$.
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: Light enters at an angle of incidence in a transparent rod of refractive index of the material of the rod. The light once entered into it will not leave it through its lateral face so, whatever be the value of angle of incidence?
explanation:
Let a light ray enters at $A$ and
refracted beam is $A B$. At the lateral
face, the angle of incidence is $\theta$. For no
refraction at this face, $\theta > C.$
$i.e.\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\sin \theta > \sin C$
but $\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\theta + r = {90^ \circ } \Rightarrow \theta = {90^ \circ } - r$
https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764950035/preptracker/questions/q_33255_explanation_1764950034720.png
$\therefore \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\sin \left( {{{90}^ \circ } - r} \right) > \sin C$
${\rm{or}}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\cos r > \sin C\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\left( 1 \right)$
By snell's law,$\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,{\kern 1pt} n = \frac{{\sin i}}{{\sin r}}$
$ \Rightarrow \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\sin r = \frac{{\sin i}}{n}$
$\therefore \,\,\,{\mkern 1mu} {\kern 1pt} \cos r = \sqrt {1 - {{\sin }^2}r} = \sqrt {\left( {1 - \frac{{{{\sin }^2}i}}{{{n^2}}}} \right)} $
From Eq. (1), $\sqrt {1 - \frac{{{{\sin }^2}i}}{{{n^2}}}} > \sin C$
Also, $\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\sin C = \frac{1}{n}$
$ \Rightarrow \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,1 - \frac{{{{\sin }^2}i}}{{{n^2}}} > \frac{1}{{{n^2}}}$
or $\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,{n^2} > {\sin ^2}i + 1$
The maximum value of $\sin i$ is 1.
$ \Rightarrow \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,{n^2} > 2\,{\rm{or}}\,n > \sqrt 2 $
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: Total internal reflection takes place
explanation: Total internal reflection takes place when ray goes from denser to rarer medium and incident angle is greaterthan critical angle.

options.text: when a ray moves from denser to rarer and incident angle is greater than critical angle
when a ray moves from rarer to denser and incident angle is less than critical angle
when a ray moves from rarer to denser and incident angle is equal to critical angle

subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: Sparkling of diamond is because of
explanation:
The brilliance of diamond is due to total internal reflection of light. $\mu$ for diamond is 2.42 , so that critical angle for diamond-air interface as calculated is $24.4^{\circ}$.
The diamond is cut suitably so that light entering the diamond from any face falls at an angle greater than $24.4^{\circ}$; suffers multiple total internal reflection at the various faces and remains with in the diamond. Hence, the diamond sparkles.
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: The critical angle for total internal reflection in diamond is $24.5^{\circ}$. The refractive index of the diamond is
explanation:
If $\mu$ is the refractive index and $C$ is
critical angle, then
$\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\mu = \frac{1}{{\sin C}}$
$ \Rightarrow \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\mu = \frac{1}{{\sin 24.5}}$
$ \Rightarrow \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\mu = 2.45$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: The refractive index of water is 1.33 . The direction in which a man under water should look to see the setting sun is
explanation:
$\sin \theta = \frac{1}{{{\mu _w}}} = \frac{1}{{1.33}}$
$ \Rightarrow \theta = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{1}{{1.33}}} \right) = {49^ \circ }$ to the vertical
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: A ray of light strikes a plane water-air interface from within the water at an angle of incidence $i$. Which one of the following statements is correct?
explanation: If $i$ is less than the critical angle,then all the light is refracted and the angle of refraction is greater than $i$.

options.text: If $i$ is ${90^ \circ },$ the angle of refraction will be the critical angle.
If $i$ is equal to the critical angle, the reflected and refracted rays will be at right angles to each other .
If $i$ is less than the critical angle, all the light is refracted and the angle of refraction is greater than $i$.
If $i$ is less than the critical angle, some light is refracted and the angle of reflection is less than the critical angle.

subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: In optical fibres, the refractive index of the core is
explanation:
In optical fibres, the refractive index of the core is greater than that of the cladding.
Thus, the core act as denser medium and cladding as rarer medium. For angle of incidence greater than critical angle, the ray forward through total internal reflection.

options.text: greater than that of the cladding
equal to that of the cladding
smaller than that of the cladding
independent of that of the cladding

subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: If the critical angle for total internal reflection from medium to vacuum is $30^{\circ}$, the velocity of light in medium is
explanation:
Refractive index of medium,
$\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\mu = \frac{1}{{\sin C}}$
where, $C$ is critical angle.
Given, $\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,C = {30^ \circ }$
$\therefore {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} \therefore \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\mu = \frac{1}{{\sin {{30}^ \circ }}} = \frac{1}{{1/2}} = 2$
From Snell's law, $\,\mu = \frac{{{v_0}}}{{{v_m}}}$
where, $v_{0}$ is speed of light in vacuum and
$v_{m}$ the velocity in medium.
$\therefore \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,{v_m} = \frac{{3 \times {{10}^8}}}{2} = 1.5 \times {10^8}\,m/s$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: Find the value of $\theta$ in the given diagram.
explanation:
https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764950033/preptracker/questions/q_33219_explanation_1764950032722.png Since, $B C$ grazes along surface
$ \Rightarrow $ angle $\left( {90 - {\theta _1}} \right) = $ critical angle $C$
$\therefore \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\sin \,C = \frac{1}{\mu }$
$\sin \left( {90 - {\theta _1}} \right) = \frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{2} \Rightarrow \cos {\theta _1} = \frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{2}$
$ \Rightarrow \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,{\kern 1pt} {\theta _1} = {30^ \circ }$
Now, using snell's law on 1st
interface,
$\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,{n_1}\sin \theta = {n_2}\sin {\theta _1}$
$ \Rightarrow \,\,{\rm{(1) }}\sin \theta = \frac{2}{{\sqrt 3 }}\sin {30^ \circ } = \frac{2}{{\sqrt 3 }} \cdot \frac{1}{2}$
$ \Rightarrow \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,{\kern 1pt} \sin \theta = \frac{1}{{\sqrt 3 }}$
$ \Rightarrow \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\theta = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{1}{{\sqrt 3 }}} \right)$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: Considering normal incidence of ray, the equivalent refractive index of combination of two slabs shown in figure is
subject: Physics

question: A transparent cube of $15\,cm$ edge contains a small air bubble. Its apparent depth when viewed through one face is $6\,cm$ and when viewed through the opposite face is $4\,cm$. Then, the refractive index of the material of the cube is
explanation:
Let real depth of the bubble from
one side is $x$ and apparent depth is 6.
Refractive index,
$\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\mu = \frac{{{\rm{Real}}\,{\rm{depth}}}}{{{\rm{Apperent}}\,{\rm{depth}}}}$
Then, $\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\mu = \frac{x}{6} = \frac{{15 - x}}{4}$
$ \Rightarrow \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,x = 9\,cm$
Hence, $\,\,\,\,\,\mu = \frac{x}{6} = \frac{9}{6} = 1.5$
So correct Option is (4).
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: A glass has refractive index $\dfrac{3}{2}$ and water has refractive index $\dfrac{4}{3}$. If the speed of light in glass is $2 \times {10^8}\,m{s^{ - 1}},$ then the speed of light in water in $m{s^{ - 1}}$ is
explanation:
Since, $\mu \nu = $constant
$\therefore \,\,\,\,\,{\mkern 1mu} {\kern 1pt} {\mu _g}{v_g} = {\mu _w}{v_w},\frac{3}{2}\left( {2 \times {{10}^8}} \right) = \frac{4}{3}{v_w}$
$ \Rightarrow \,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,{v_w} = 2.25 \times {10^8}\,m{s^{ - 1}}$
subject: Physics

question: The frequency of a light wave in a material is $2 \times {10^{14}}\,Hz$ and wavelength is $5000\mathop A\limits^ \circ  .$ The refractive index of material will be
subject: Physics

question: A beam of light composed of red and green rays is incident obliquely at a point on the face of a rectangular glass slab. When coming out on the opposite parallel face, the red and green rays emerge from
explanation: In any medium other than air or vacuum, the velocities of different colours are different. Therefore, both red and green colours are refracted at different angles of refractions. Hence, after emerging from glass slab through opposite parallel face, they appear at two different points and move in two different parallel directions.

options.text: two points propagating in two different non-parallel directions
two points propagating in two different parallel directions
one point propagating in two different directions
one point propagating in the same direction

subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question: The optical path of a monochromatic light is same, if it goes through $4\,cm$ of glass or $4.5\,cm$ of water. If the refractive index of glass is 1.53 , the refractive index of the water is
subject: Physics

question: When a light ray enters from oil to glass on oil- glass interface, then velocity of light changes by a factor of $\left[ {{\rm{given}},{\mu _{{\rm{oil }}}} = 2\,{\rm{and}}\,{\mu _{{\rm{glass }}}} = 3/2} \right]$
subject: Physics

question: If frequency $(v)$ of light is $5 \times {10^{16}}\,Hz$ and speed of light in air is $3 \times {10^8}\,m/s.$ Find the ratio of wavelength of light in a medium of refractive index 2 to air.
explanation:
$v=v \lambda$, where $v$ is same for a source
of light.
$ \Rightarrow \,\,\,\,\,\,v \propto \lambda ,\frac{{{v_1}}}{{{v_2}}} = \frac{{{\lambda _1}}}{{{\lambda _2}}}{\rm{ }}$
But ${v_1} = \frac{c}{{{n_1}}}$ and ${v_2}$ (in air) $ = \frac{c}{{{n_2}}} = c$
$ \Rightarrow \,\,\,\frac{{{\lambda _1}}}{{{\lambda _2}}} = \frac{{c/{n_1}}}{c} = \frac{1}{{{n_1}}} = \frac{1}{2}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,{\mkern 1mu} {\kern 1pt} \left[ {\because {n_1} = 2} \right]$
subject: Physics

question: If velocity of light in air is $c$, what will be the velocity of light in a medium of refractive index 1.4 ?
explanation:
Refractive index of medium
$\, = \frac{{{\rm{ Speed}}\,{\rm{of}}\,{\rm{light}}\,{\rm{in}}\,{\rm{vacuum }}}}{{{\rm{ Speed}}\,{\rm{in}}\,{\rm{medium }}}}$
$ \Rightarrow \,\,\,{\rm{Speed}}\,{\rm{of}}\,{\rm{light}}\,{\rm{in}}\,{\rm{medium}}$
$ = \frac{{{\rm{ Speed}}\,{\rm{of}}\,{\rm{light}}\,{\rm{in}}\,{\rm{vacuum }}}}{{{\rm{ Refractive}}\,{\rm{index }}}}$
$ \Rightarrow v = \frac{c}{n} = \frac{{3 \times {8^8}}}{{1.4}}$
$ = 2.14 \times {10^8}\,m/s$
So correct option is (1).
subject: Physics

question: A ray of light falls on a transparent glass slab of refractive index ${\sqrt{3}}$. If the reflected and refracted ray are at ${90^{\circ}}$ to each other, then the angle of incidence is
explanation:
https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764955638/preptracker/questions/q_33147_explanation_1764955638150.png $r+r^{\prime}=90^{\circ}$
So, ${m=\tan \theta}$
$\sqrt{3}=\tan \theta$
$\theta=60^{\circ}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question: A coin is placed at the bottom of a tank which is filled by two liquid layers as shown. The apparent depth of coin from top of surface for normal viewing is
explanation:
${A=\dfrac{R_{1}}{\mu_{1}}+\dfrac{R_{2}}{\mu_{2}}}$
$\begin{aligned}& =\dfrac{12}{4 / 3}+\dfrac{6}{3 / 2} \\& =9+4=13 {~cm}\end{aligned}$
subject: Physics

question: An air bubble in glass slab ${(\mu=1.5)}$ when viewed from one side appears to be at $3\,cm$ and from opposite side at $4\,cm$. The thickness of the slab is
subject: Physics

question: When the rectangular metal tank is filled up to the top with an unknown liquid, an observer with eyes level with the top of the tank can just see the corner ${E}$. Find the index of refraction of the liquid
explanation:
In ${\triangle A B C}$
${\operatorname{Sin} \theta_{c}=\dfrac{60}{\sqrt{60^{2}+80^{2}}}=\dfrac{60}{100}}$
https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764955625/preptracker/questions/q_33080_explanation_1764955625370.png
Also, ${\sin \theta_{C}=\dfrac{1}{\mu}}$
${\Rightarrow \dfrac{60}{100}=\dfrac{1}{\mu}\left[\because \operatorname{Sin} \theta c=\dfrac{100}{60}\right] \Rightarrow \mu=1.67}$
subject: Physics

question: A vessel of depth ${t}$ is half filled with oil of refractive index ${\mu_{1}}$ and the other half is filled with water of refractive index ${\mu_{2}}$. The apperant depth of vessel when viewed from above is
explanation:
https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764950006/preptracker/questions/q_33076_explanation_1764950006560.png The apparent depth will be sum of depth due water and oil
${=\dfrac{t_{1}}{\mu_{1}}+\dfrac{t_{2}}{\mu_{2}}=\dfrac{t}{2}\left[\dfrac{1}{\mu_{1}}+\dfrac{1}{\mu_{2}}\right]=\dfrac{t\left(\mu_{1}+\mu_{2}\right)}{2 \mu_{1} \mu_{2}}}$
subject: Physics

question: A point source of light ${S}$ is placed at the bottom of a vessel containing a liquid of refractive index ${5 / 3}$. A person is viewing the source from above the surface. There is an opaque disc ${D}$ of radius $1\,cm$ floating on the surface of the liquid. The centre of the disc lies vertically above the source. The liquid from the vessel is gradually drained out through a tap. The maximum height of the liquid for which the source cannot be seen at all from above is
explanation:
Suppose the maximum height of the liquid is ${h}$ for which the source is not visible.
Hence radius of the disc https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764956785/preptracker/questions/q_33072_explanation_1764956784147.png We have,
${\sin i_{c}=\dfrac{r}{\sqrt{r^{2}+h^{2}}}}$
${\Rightarrow \dfrac{1}{\mu}=\dfrac{r}{\sqrt{r^{2}+h^{2}}}}$
$\begin{aligned}& \Rightarrow \dfrac{1}{\mu^{2}}=\dfrac{r^{2}}{r^{2}+h^{2}} \\& \Rightarrow r=\dfrac{h}{\sqrt{\mu^{2}-1}}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned}& \Rightarrow 1=\dfrac{h}{\left(\dfrac{5}{3}\right)^{2}-1} \\& \Rightarrow h=1.33 {~cm}\end{aligned}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: A medium shows relation between ${i}$ and ${r}$ as shown. If speed of light in the medium is ${(n c)}$ then value of ${n}$ is
explanation:
As ${y=m x+c}$
${\Rightarrow \sin r=\left(\tan 30^{\circ}\right) \sin i+0}$
$ \Rightarrow \tan 30^\circ = \frac{{\sin r}}{{\sin i}}$
$ \Rightarrow \frac{1}{{\sqrt 3 }} = \frac{{\sin r}}{{\sin i}} = \frac{1}{\mu } \Rightarrow \mu = \sqrt 3 $
Also ${v=\dfrac{c}{\mu}=n c \Rightarrow n=\dfrac{1}{\mu}=\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{3}}=(3)^{-1 / 2}}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question: Find the time taken by a monochromatic light to cross a transparent slab of width ${2 m}$ and  $R.I. = \sqrt 2 $  as shown in figure
explanation:
https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764950004/preptracker/questions/q_33064_explanation_1764950004149.png By Snell's law, we have
${\Rightarrow \sqrt{2} \sin \phi=\sin 45^{\circ}}$
${\Rightarrow \operatorname{Sin} \phi=1 / 2}$
${\Rightarrow \phi=30^{\circ}}$
So, ${t=\dfrac{A B \cos \phi}{c / \sqrt{2}}=\dfrac{\sqrt{2} A B / \cos \phi}{c}}$
$ = \frac{{\sqrt 2 AB/\cos 30^\circ }}{c} = \frac{{\sqrt 2 \times 2 \times 2}}{{c \times \sqrt 3 }}$
$ = \frac{{4\sqrt 2 }}{{\sqrt 3 \times 3 \times {{10}^8}}}$
${=\dfrac{4 \sqrt{2}}{3 \sqrt{3}} \times 10^{-8} {~m} / {s}}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question: A bubble in glass slab ${[\mu=1.5]}$ when viewed from one side appears at $5\,cm$ and $2\,cm$ from other side then thickness of slab is
explanation:
Let ' ${t_{1}}$ ' and ' ${t_{2}}$ ' be the real depth of bubble from both side https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764950001/preptracker/questions/q_33060_explanation_1764950001247.png So, ${\dfrac{t_{1}}{\mu}=5 {~cm}}$ and ${\dfrac{t_{2}}{\mu}=2 {~cm}}$
${\Rightarrow t_{1}=5 \mu {cm}}$ and ${t_{2}=2 \mu {cm}}$
${\Rightarrow t_{1}+t_{2}=(5 \mu+2 \mu) {cm}}$
${=7 \mu {cm}=7 \times 1.5 {~cm}=10.5 {~cm}}$
subject: Physics

question:
Read the Assertion and Reason carefully to mark the correct options given below:

Assertion: The phase difference of two light waves change if they travel through different media having same thickness, but different indices of refraction.

Reason: The wavelengths of waves are different in different media.
explanation:
The velocities and hence wavelengths of waves are different in different media, that is why, the phase difference of two light wave change if they travel through different media.
Hence, both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are correct but Reason is  NOT  the correct explanation of  Assertion.
Assertion is not correct but Reason is correct.
Both Assertion and  Reason  are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of Assertion.
Assertion is correct but Reason is not correct.

subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question: A microscope is focused on an object at the bottom of a bucket. If liquid with refractive index $5 / 3$ is poured inside the bucket, then microscope have to be raised by $30\,cm$ to focus the object again. 'The height of the liquid in the bucket is
explanation:
Let before the pouring liquid the height $ = h\,cm$
When object is in denser medium and it is observed from rarer medium, then for near normal vision, $\mu = \frac{{{\rm{ Real}}\,\,{\rm{depth }}}}{{{\rm{ Apparent}}\,{\rm{ depth }}}}$.
$h^{\prime}=\dfrac{h}{\mu}$ (Apparent depth object)
Now, using
${y^d} = h - h' = h\left( {1 - \frac{1}{\mu }} \right) = 30$
https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764949981/preptracker/questions/q_32984_explanation_1764949980788.png $h\left(1-\dfrac{3}{5}\right)=30$
$h = \frac{{5 \times 30}}{2} = 75\,cm$
subject: Physics

question: The critical angle for a denser-rarer interface is  $45^\circ .$  The speed of light in rarer medium is $3 \times {10^8}\,m/s$. The speed of light in the denser medium is
explanation:
$i_{C}=$ Critical angle $\Rightarrow \sin i_{C}=\dfrac{1}{\mu}$
$\dfrac{v}{c}=\dfrac{1}{\mu}=\sin i_{C}=\sin 45^{\circ}=\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$
$ \Rightarrow v = \frac{c}{{\sqrt 2 }} = \frac{{3 \times {{10}^8}}}{{\sqrt 2 }}\,m/s$
$ = 2.12 \times {10^8}\,m/s$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: A monochromatic light wave with wavelength $\lambda_{1}$ and frequency $v_{1}$ in air enters another medium. If the angle of incidence and angle of refraction at the interface are $45^{\circ}$ and $30^{\circ}$ respectively, then the wavelength $\lambda_{2}$ and frequency $v_{2}$ of the refracted wave are
explanation:
$\mu_{1} \sin i=\mu_{2} \sin r$ (Using Snell's law)
$\Rightarrow \mu_{1} \sin 45^{\circ}=\mu_{2} \sin 30^{\circ} \Rightarrow \dfrac{\mu_{1}}{\mu_{2}}=\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$
$\Rightarrow \dfrac{\mu_{1}}{\mu_{2}}=\dfrac{\lambda_{2}}{\lambda_{1}}=\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \Rightarrow \lambda_{2}=\dfrac{\lambda_{1}}{\sqrt{2}}$
Frequency does not change on changing the medium.
subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question: A vessel of depth ' $d$ ' is half filled with oil of refractive index $n_{1}$ and the other half is filled with water of refractive index $n_{2}$. The apparent depth of this vessel when viewed from above will be
explanation:
https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764949977/preptracker/questions/q_32904_explanation_1764949976658.png Apparent depth for a system of multi liquid layers
$=\dfrac{t_{1}}{n_{1}}+\dfrac{t_{2}}{n_{2}}$ $=\dfrac{d / 2}{n_{1}}+\dfrac{d / 2}{n_{2}}=\dfrac{d\left(n_{1}+n_{2}\right)}{2 n_{1} n_{2}}$
subject: Physics

question: An ice cube has a bubble inside. When viewed from one side the apparent distance of the bubble is $12\,cm$. When viewed from the opposite side, the apparent distance of the bubble is observed as $4\,cm$. If the side of the ice cube is $24\,cm$, the refractive index of the ice cube is
explanation:
https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764949975/preptracker/questions/q_32898_explanation_1764949975087.png Apparent depth $=($ real depth $) / \mu$.
From one side
$x/\mu = 12\,cm\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\left( 1 \right)$
From opposite side
$(24 - x)/\mu = 4\,cm\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\left( 2 \right)$
Divide (1) by (2), we get
$\dfrac{x / \mu}{(24-x) / \mu}=\dfrac{12}{4} \Rightarrow \dfrac{x}{24-x}=3 \Rightarrow x=18$
$\mu=\dfrac{18}{12}=\dfrac{3}{2}$
subject: Physics

question:
Assertion: If optical density of a substance is more than that of water then the mass density of substance can be less than water.

Reason: Optical density and mass density are not related.
explanation: Optical density and mass density are distinct concepts. Mass density is defined as mass per unit volume and is not dependent on optical properties. It is possible for the mass density of a substance to be less than that of water even if its optical density is greater. For instance, turpentine has a lower mass density than water, even though its optical density is higher due to the difference in the speed of light in the two media. So correct option is (1).

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are correct but Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is correct but Reason is incorrect.
Assertion is incorrect but reason is correct.

subject: Physics
subtopic: Miscellaneous Problems on Refraction at Plane Surface

question:
Assertion: A ray of white light shows no dispersion on emerging from a glass slab although there occurs dispersion inside the glass slab.

Reason: The velocity of light inside the glass slab is same for all different colours.
explanation: When light enters a glass slab, it disperses into different colors because the velocity of light varies for each color. However, upon emerging from the parallel face of the slab, the rays of different colors reconverge in the same direction, to reconstitute white light. So correct option is (3).

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are correct but Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is correct but Reason is incorrect.
Assertion is incorrect but reason is correct.

subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question:
Assertion: Air is rarer than glass.

Reason: The light travelling from air to glass can not suffer total internal reflection.
explanation: Light transitioning from air to glass cannot undergo total internal reflection because the crucial requirement for total internal reflection is that light must travel from a denser medium to a less dense medium, with an incident angle greater than the critical angle. (They are different facts) So correct option is (2).

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are correct but Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is correct but Reason is incorrect.
Assertion is incorrect but reason is correct.

subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question:
Assertion: If refractive index of one medium is equal to refractive index of second medium, then beam does not bend at all.

Reason: The bending of light does not depend on refractive indices of media.
explanation: Here, bending means deviation in original path. $\mu_{2}=\dfrac{\operatorname{Sin} i}{\operatorname{Sin} r}$ (where subscripts 1 and 2 are for two media) If refractive index of one medium is equal to refractive index of the second medium, then the beam does not bend at all. $\operatorname{Sin} i=\operatorname{Sin} r \Rightarrow i=r$ bending of light depends on the refractive indices of the media. So correct option is (3).

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are correct but Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is correct but Reason is incorrect.
Assertion is incorrect but reason is correct.

subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question:
Assertion: The frequencies of incident, reflected and refracted beam of monochromatic light incident from one medium to another are same.

Reason: The incident, reflected and refracted rays are coplanar.
explanation: The frequency of light is a measure of energy levels of the light source, and it remains constant regardless of the medium through which it propagates. Reason is a separate fact. So correct option is (2).

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are correct but Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is correct but Reason is incorrect.
Assertion is incorrect but reason is correct.

subject: Physics
subtopic: Miscellaneous Problems on Refraction at Plane Surface

question:
Assertion: Higher is the refractive index of a medium or denser the medium, lesser is the velocity of light in that medium.

Reason: Refractive index is inversely proportional to velocity.
explanation:
$\mu=\dfrac{c}{v} \propto \dfrac{1}{v}$
$(\because c=$ constant $)$
High value of $\mu$ is for 'denser' medium. So correct option is (1).

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are correct but Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is correct but Reason is incorrect.
Assertion is incorrect but reason is correct.

question:
Assertion: The colour of the green flower seen through red glass appears to be dark.

Reason: Red glass transmits only red light.
explanation: Red glass transmits only red (and absorbs other colors). The green light from green flower is absorbed by red glass and so the flower appears to be black. So correct option is (1).

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are correct but Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is correct but Reason is incorrect.
Assertion is incorrect but reason is correct.

question:
Assertion: Optical fibre follows total internal reflection.

Reason: In optical fibre, the diameter of the core is kept small.
explanation: The smaller diameter of the core in optical fibers ensures that the fiber has an incident angle greater than the critical angle, facilitating total internal reflection, which is correctly explained in the reason. So correct option is (1).

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are correct but Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.

question:
Assertion: An empty test tube dipped into water in a beaker appears silver, when viewed from a suitable direction.

Reason: Due to refraction of light. the substance in water appears silvery.
explanation: Total internal reflection ($T I R$) occurs when light strikes the water-air interface with an angle of incidence becomes greater than the critical angle, leading to a silvery sheen in the test tube when viewed from a specific direction. In $T I R$ there is no loss of energy. So refraction is not cause. So correct option is (3).

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are correct but Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is correct but Reason is incorrect.
Assertion is incorrect but reason is correct.

subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question:
Assertion: There is no dispersion of light refracted through a rectangular glass slab.

Reason: Light is completely absorbed by glass slab.
explanation: Refraction through two parallel faces of a glass slab causes white light to emerge parallel to its original incidence, So, there is no dispersion but only lateral displacement. It is not absorbed fully. So correct option is (3).

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are correct but Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is correct but Reason is incorrect.
Assertion is incorrect but reason is correct.

question:
Assertion: The images formed by total internal reflections are much brighter than those formed by mirrors or lenses.

Reason: There is no loss of intensity in total internal refraction.
explanation:
Total internal reflection (TIR) retains $100 \%$ of incident light without intensity loss, creating
$\Rightarrow$ Brighter images are formed by TIR (compared to reflection from mirrors or refraction through lenses, which involve absorption and so their images are less intense). So correct option is (1).

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are correct but Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is correct but Reason is incorrect.
Assertion is incorrect but reason is correct.

subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: The critical angle of light going from medium $A$ to medium $B$ is $\theta$. The speed of light in medium $A$ is $v$. The speed of light in medium $B$ is:
explanation: $\sin \theta=\dfrac{1}{\mu}=\dfrac{C_{A}}{C_{B}} \Rightarrow C_{B}=\dfrac{V}{\sin \theta}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: In vacuum, to travel distance $d$, light takes time $t$ and in medium to travel $5\,d$, it takes time $T$. The critical angle of the medium is:
explanation:
In vacuum $c=d / t$
In medium $v=\dfrac{5 d}{T}$
$\mu=\dfrac{c}{v}=\dfrac{T}{5 t}$
Also, $\sin C=\dfrac{1}{\mu}$
( $C$ is critical angle)
$\therefore C=\sin ^{-1}\left(\dfrac{5 t}{T}\right) \text {. }$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: For the given incident ray as shown in figure, the condition of total internal refraction of this ray the minimum refractive index of prism will be :
explanation:
$\mu_{2}=\sqrt{\mu_{1}+\sin ^{2} i}$
$\begin{aligned}& \mu_{2}=\sqrt{1+\sin ^{2} 45} \\& \mu_{2}=\sqrt{\dfrac{3}{2}} .\end{aligned}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: A light wave travels from glass to water. The refractive index for glass and water are $4/3$ and $3/2$ respectively. The value of the critical angle will be:
explanation:
Here water is denser and glass is rarer.
$\begin{aligned}& \dfrac{3}{2} \sin C=\dfrac{4}{3} \sin 90^{\circ} \\& \Rightarrow C=\sin ^{-1}\left(\dfrac{8}{9}\right) .\end{aligned}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: Light travels in two media $A$ and $B$ with speeds $1.8 \times {10^8}\,m{s^{ - 1}}$ and $2.4 \times {10^8}\,m{s^{ - 1}}$ respectively. Then, the critical angle between $A$ and $B$ :
explanation:
If $C$ is critical angle then, $\sin C=\dfrac{v_{1}}{v_{2}}$
$C = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{{v_1}}}{{{v_2}}}} \right) = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{1.8 \times {{10}^8}}}{{2.4 \times {{10}^8}}}} \right)$
$ = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{3}{4}} \right).$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: There are certain materials developed in laboratories which have a negative refractive index. A ray incident from air (Medium 1) into such a medium (Medium 2) shall follow a path given by:
explanation: The negative refractive index materials are those in which ray is incident from air (Medium 1) on those material, the ray is refracted on the same side of the normal.
subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question: For a colour of light the wavelength in air is $6000\,\mathop {{\rm{ }}A}\limits^{\,\,^\circ } $ and in water the wavelength is $4500\,\mathop {{\rm{ }}A}\limits^{\,\,^\circ } $. Then the speed of light in water will be:
explanation:
$v \propto \lambda \Rightarrow \dfrac{v_{1}}{v_{2}}=\dfrac{\lambda_{1}}{\lambda_{2}}$
$\therefore v_{2}=\dfrac{\lambda_{2}}{\lambda_{21}} \times v_{1}=\dfrac{4500}{6000} \times 3 \times 10^{8}$
${v_2} = 2.25 \times {10^8}\,m{\rm{/}}s$.
subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question: Monochromatic light is refracted from air into glass of refractive index $\mu$. The ratio of the wavelength of incident and refracted waves is:
explanation:
As we know,
$\begin{aligned}& \lambda \propto \dfrac{1}{\mu} \\& \dfrac{\lambda_{1}}{\lambda_{2}}=\dfrac{\mu_{2}}{\mu_{1}}=\dfrac{\mu}{1} \\& \dfrac{\lambda_{1}}{\lambda_{2}}=\dfrac{\mu}{1} .\end{aligned}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question: In a vessel two immiscible liquids are filled as shown in the figure. The effective refractive index of the combination for normal view is
explanation:
$d_{A P P}=\dfrac{d_{1}}{\mu_{1}}+\dfrac{d_{2}}{\mu_{2}}=\dfrac{d_{1}+d_{2}}{\mu_{\text {eff }}}$
$\Rightarrow \mu_{e f f}=\dfrac{d_{1}+d_{2}}{\dfrac{d_{1}}{\mu_{1}}+\dfrac{d_{2}}{\mu_{2}}}$.
subject: Physics

question: $\quad T$ is a point at the bottom of a tank filled with water, as shown. The redfractive index of water is $4 / 3 . Y P T$ is the vertical line through $T$. To an observer at the position $O, T$ will appear to be:
explanation:
The apparent depth of the point $T$ is
$\dfrac{t}{u}=\dfrac{4}{4 / 3}=3 m$
Correct option is (3).
subject: Physics

question: A ray of light passes from vacuum into a medium of refractive index $\mu$, the angle of incidence is found to be twice the angle of refraction. Then the angle of incidence is
explanation:
$\mu = \frac{{\sin i}}{{\sin r}}$
Given $i = 2r$
$\mu = 2\,\cos \,r$
$\Rightarrow \mu=\dfrac{\sin 2 r}{\sin r}=\dfrac{2 \sin r \cdot \cos r}{\sin r}$ https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764949955/preptracker/questions/q_32515_explanation_1764949955268.jpg $r=\cos ^{-1}\left(\dfrac{\mu}{2}\right)$,
$\therefore i=2 \cos ^{-1}\left(\dfrac{\mu}{2}\right)$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question:
Assertion: Endoscopy involves use of optical fibres to study internal organs.

Reason: Optical fibres are based on phenomenon of total internal reflection.
explanation:
Endoscopy involves the use of optical fibers to study internal organs. This is correct. Endoscopy is a medical procedure that utilizes thin, flexible optical fibers with a light source and a camera to visualize and study internal organs or cavities.
The Reason provided explains why optical fibers are used in endoscopy. It correctly states that optical fibers are based on the phenomenon of total internal reflection. This phenomenon allows light to be transmitted through the fibers by repeatedly undergoing total internal reflection, which enables the transmission of images and illumination in endoscopy procedures.
The Reason provides a valid and accurate explanation for the Assertion, so the correct choice is (1).

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are correct but Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is correct but Reason is incorrect.
Assertion is incorrect but reason is correct.

subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: A fish looking up through the water sees the outside world, contained in a circular horizon. If the refractive index of water is $\dfrac{4}{3}$ and the fish is $12\,cm$ below the water surface, the radius of this circle in $cm$ is
explanation:
The situtaiton is shown in figure https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764949950/preptracker/questions/q_32472_explanation_1764949949756.png $\begin{aligned}& \sin \theta_{C}=\dfrac{1}{\mu} \Rightarrow \tan \theta_{C}=\dfrac{A B}{A O}\left(\Rightarrow \sin \theta_{c}=\dfrac{3}{4}\right) \\& \Rightarrow A B=O A \tan \theta_{C}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{gathered}A B=\dfrac{O A}{\sqrt{\mu^{2}-1}}=\dfrac{12}{\sqrt{\left(\dfrac{4}{3}\right)^{2}-1}}=\dfrac{36}{\sqrt{7}} \\\quad\left[\therefore \tan \theta_{C}=\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{\mu^{2}-1}}\right]\end{gathered}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question:
Assertion: Diamond glitters brilliantly.

Reason: Diamond does not absorb sunlight.
explanation:
Diamond glitters brilliantly because light enters in diamond suffers total internal reflection. So all the light enters in it comes out after number of reflections and no light is observed by it.
So, both the Assertion and Reason are correct, but the Reason does not provide the correct explanation for the brilliance of diamond. Diamond's brilliance is more related to its high refractive index and its ability to refract and disperse light effectively.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are correct but Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is correct but Reason is incorrect.
Both Assertion and Reason are incorrect.

subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: The refractive index of glass is 1.5 . The speed of light in glass is
subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question: A ray of light is incident on the surface of a glass plate of thickness $t$. If the angle of incidence $\theta$ is small, the emerging ray would be displaced side ways by an amount (take, $n=$ refractive index of glass)
explanation:
If $\theta$ is small $\sin \theta \cong \theta$ and $\cos \theta \cong 1$
$\Rightarrow \mu=\dfrac{\sin i}{\sin r}=\dfrac{i}{r} \Rightarrow r=\dfrac{i}{\mu}$
$x=\dfrac{t \sin (i-r)}{\cos r}=\dfrac{t(i-r)}{1}$
$\Rightarrow t i-\dfrac{i}{\mu}=t i\left(\dfrac{\mu-1}{\mu}\right)$
$i=\theta, \mu=n$
$\Rightarrow x=\dfrac{t \theta(n-1)}{n}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question: The apparent depth of water in cylindrical water tank of diameter $2{\mkern 1mu} R\,cm$ is reducing at the rate of $x\,cm\,{\rm{mi}}{{\rm{n}}^{ - 1}}$ when water is being drained out at a constant rate. The amount of water drained in cc per minute is ( $n_{1}=$ refractive index of air and $n_{2}=$ refractive index of water)
explanation:
Let actual height of water in cylindrical tank be $h$.
$\dfrac{n_{2}}{n_{1}}=\dfrac{\text { Actual depth }}{\text { Apparent depth }}$
or $\dfrac{n_{2}}{n_{1}}=\dfrac{h}{x}$
or $\dfrac{n_{2}}{n_{1}}=\dfrac{\dfrac{d h}{d t}}{\dfrac{d x}{d t}}$
$\therefore \dfrac{d h}{d t}=\dfrac{n_{2}}{n_{1}} \times \dfrac{d x}{d t}$
or Change in actual rate of flow
$=\dfrac{n_{2}}{n_{1}} \times$ Change in apparent rate of flow
or $\frac{{dh}}{{dt}} = \frac{{{n_2}}}{{{n_1}}} \times x\,cm\,{\rm{mi}}{{\rm{n}}^{ - 1}}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\left( 1 \right)$
Thus, amount of water drained in cc per min
$M_{0}=\dfrac{d h}{d t} \times \pi R^{2}$
${M_0} = \left[ {\frac{{{n_2}}}{{{n_1}}} \times x \times \pi {R^2}} \right]$ $\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,{\rm{[From (1)]}}$
$ \Rightarrow {M_0} = \left[ {x\pi {R^2}\frac{{{n_2}}}{{{n_1}}}} \right]c{m^3}/\min $
subject: Physics

question: For a given pair of transparent media, the critical angle for which colour is maximum?
explanation:
Red $(R)$ has maximum critical angle in visible light. $\lambda_{R}>\lambda_{V}$ (where subscript $V$ is for Violet)
$\mu_{R} < \mu_{V}\left(\because \mu=\dfrac{c}{v}, v=f \lambda\right)$
$\sin \theta_{c}=\dfrac{1}{\mu}$
$\Rightarrow\left(\theta_{c}\right)_{\text {Red }}>\left(\theta_{c}\right)_{\text {violet }}$.
Correct option is (4).
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: A ray of light is incident at an angle $i$ from denser to rarer medium. The reflected and the refracted rays are mutually perpendicular. The angle of reflection and the angle of refraction are respectively $r$ and $r'$ , then the critical angle will be
explanation:
Let ${\mu _1}\,\& \,{\mu _2}$ are the refractive indices of denser and rarer mediums respectively.
${\mu _1}\sin i = {\mu _2}\sin r'\,\,\,\,\,\,(1)$
The critical angle is
${\mu _1}\sin C = {\mu _2}\sin 90^\circ $
$ \Rightarrow \frac{{{\mu _1}}}{{{\mu _2}}} = \frac{1}{{\sin C}}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,(2)$
From (1) & (2)
$\frac{1}{{\sin C}} = \frac{{\sin r'}}{{\sin r}} = \frac{{\sin (90 - r)}}{{\sin r}} = \frac{{\cos r}}{{\sin r}}$
$\sin C = \tan r = \tan i\quad (i = r)$
$C = {\sin ^{ - 1}}(\tan i)$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question:
A layer of ice \[\left( {\mu = 1.33} \right)\] lies on a glass plate \[\left( {\mu = 1.5} \right)\]. A ray of light makes an angle of incidence \[60^\circ \] on the surface of ice as shown in the figure below.
Match column I with column II
A. $\sin \,{r_1}$
B. $\sin \,{r_2}$
C . Refractive index of ice with respect to glass
P. $\left( {1{\rm{/}}\sqrt 3 } \right)$
Q. $\left( {3{\rm{/}}\sqrt 3 } \right){\rm{/}}8$
R. $8{\rm{/}}9$
explanation:
In this case, the light gets refracted twice Air-ice interface. https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764949946/preptracker/questions/q_32426_explanation_1764949946432.png From Snell’s law
$\frac{{\sin \,60^\circ }}{{\sin \,{r_1}}} = \frac{{{\mu _{ice}}}}{1}$
$ \Rightarrow \sin {\mkern 1mu} {r_1} = \frac{{\sin {\mkern 1mu} 60^\circ }}{{{\mu _{ice}}}} = \frac{{\sqrt 3 /2}}{{4/3}} = \frac{{3\sqrt 3 }}{8}\,\,\,\,\,\,(1)$
At Ice-glass interface
From eq’s (1) and (2)
${\mu _{Ice}}\sin {r_1} = {\mu _{glass}}\sin {r_2}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,(2)$
From eq’s (1) and (2)
$ \Rightarrow \sin \,{r_2} = \frac{{\sin 60^\circ }}{{{\mu _{glass}}}} = \frac{{\left( {\sqrt 3 /2} \right)}}{{1.5}} = \frac{{\sqrt 3 /2}}{{3/2}} = \left( {\frac{1}{{\sqrt 3 }}} \right)$
Also, refractive index of ice w.r.t. glass
$ \Rightarrow {\mu _{ice,glass}} = \frac{{{\mu _{ice}}}}{{{\mu _{glass}}}} = \frac{{4/3}}{{3/2}} = \frac{8}{9}$
So, the correct option is (2)

options.text: A - P, B - Q, C - R
A - Q, B - P, C - R
A - Q, B - R, C - P
A - Q, B - R, C - P

subject: Physics

question: A rectangular glass slab $ABCD$ of refractive index ${\mu _1}$ is immersed in water of refractive index ${\mu _2}\left( {{\mu _1} > {\mu _2}} \right).$ A ray of light is incident at the surface $AD{\rm{ }}$ of the slab as shown. The maximum value of the angle of incidence ${\alpha _{\max }},$ such that the ray comes out only from the other surface $BC$ is given by
explanation:
The ray comes out from $CD,$ means rays after refraction from $AD$ get total internal reflection at $DC$
According to Snell’s law https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764949945/preptracker/questions/q_32422_explanation_1764949944767.png $\frac{{{\mu _1}}}{{{\mu _2}}} = \frac{{\sin {\alpha _{\max }}}}{{\sin {r_1}}}$
$ \Rightarrow {\alpha _{\max }} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{{\mu _1}}}{{{\mu _2}}}\sin {r_1}} \right]\,\,\,\,\,\,(1)$
$ \Rightarrow $${r_1} = 90 - C$
$ \Rightarrow \,{r_1} = {90^0} - {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{{{\mu _2}}}{{{\mu _1}}}} \right)\,\,\,\,\,\,(2)$
Then, from Eqs. (1) and (2), we get
${\alpha _{\max }} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{{\mu _1}}}{{{\mu _2}}}\sin \left( {{{90}^0} - {{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{{{\mu _2}}}{{{\mu _1}}}} \right)} \right]$
$ = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left[ {\frac{{{\mu _1}}}{{{\mu _2}}}\cos \left( {{{\sin }^{ - 1}}\frac{{{\mu _2}}}{{{\mu _1}}}} \right)} \right]$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: With respect to air critical angle in a medium for light of red colour $\left[ {{\lambda _1}} \right]$ is  $\theta $ Other facts remaining same, critical angle for light of yellow colour $\left[ {{\lambda _2}} \right]$  will be
explanation:
Critical angle $ = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{1}{\mu }} \right)$
$\therefore $ ${\rm{\theta }} = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{1}{{{\mu _{{\lambda _1}}}}}} \right)$ and ${\rm{\theta }}' = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{1}{{{\mu _{{\lambda _2}}}}}} \right)$
Since ${\mu _{{\lambda _2}}} > {\mu _{{\lambda _1}}}\,,$ hence ${\rm{\theta }}' < {\rm{\theta }}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: A beam of light of converging towards a point $I$ on a screen. A plane glass plate whose thickness in the direction of the beam = t, Refractive index =$\mu $ , is introduced in the path of the beam. The convergence point is shifted by
explanation: Normal https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764949943/preptracker/questions/q_32415_explanation_1764949942677.png  shift $\Delta x = \left( {1 - \frac{1}{\mu }} \right)t$ and shift takes place in direction of ray.
subject: Physics

question: A glass slab of thickness $3\,cm$ and refractive index 1.5 is placed in front of a concave mirror. What is the radius of curvature of the mirror, so that the image coincides with the object?
explanation:
If the object and image have to coincide then the apparent distance of the object w.r.to the mirror should be equal to radius of curvature of the mirror. https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764955549/preptracker/questions/q_32411_explanation_1764955548540.png The apparent distance is
$ = \left[ {\frac{{10cm}}{1} + \frac{{3cm}}{{1.5}}} \right] + 5cm$
$R = 10cm + 2cm + 5cm = 17cm$
subject: Physics

question: A plane mirror is placed at the bottom of a tank containing a liquid of refractive index $\mu $. $P$ is a small object at a height $h$above the mirror. An observer $O$ vertically above $P$ outside the liquid sees $P$ and its image in a mirror. The apparent distance between these two will be
explanation:
As, $P$ is at a height $h$ above the mirror, image of $P$ will be at a depth $h$ below the mirror.
If $d$ is depth of liquid in the tank, apparent depth of P,
${x_1} = \frac{{d - h}}{\mu }$
Apparent depth of image of $P$ is ${x_2} = \frac{{d + h}}{\mu }$
$\therefore $Apparent distance between $P$ and its image
$ = {x_2} - {x_1} = \frac{{d + h}}{\mu } - \frac{{d - h}}{\mu } = \frac{{2h}}{\mu }$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: Considering normal incidence of ray, the equivalent refractive index of combination of two slabs shown in figure is
explanation:
Let $\mu $ is the equivalent refractive index of the combination. the apparent depth of the combination is
$\frac{{{t_1}}}{{{\mu _1}}} + \frac{{{t_2}}}{{{\mu _2}}} = \frac{{{t_1} + {t_2}}}{\mu }$
$\frac{{10}}{{\frac{4}{3}}} + \frac{{15}}{{\frac{3}{2}}} = \frac{{25}}{\mu }$
$\mu = 1.43$
subject: Physics

question: A ray of light is incident on a medium with angle of incidence \[i\] and refracted into a second medium with angle of refraction \[r\]. The graph of sin(\[i\]) vs sin(\[r\]) is as shown in figure. Then, the velocity of light in the first medium is \[n\] times the velocity of light in the second medium. What should be the value of \[n\]?
explanation:
From Snell’s law,
${\mu _1}\sin i = {\mu _2}\sin r \Rightarrow \frac{{{\mu _2}}}{{{\mu _1}}} = \frac{{\sin i}}{{\sin r}}$
Where ${\mu _1}\,and\,{\mu _2}$ are the refractive indices. From the graph,
$\tan {60^o} = \frac{{\sin i}}{{\sin r}} \Rightarrow \frac{{\sin i}}{{\sin r}} = \sqrt 3 = \frac{{{\mu _2}}}{{{\mu _1}}}$
$\mu = \frac{c}{v} \Rightarrow \, = \frac{{{\mu _2}}}{{{\mu _1}}} = \frac{{{v_1}}}{{{v_2}}}$
${v_1} = \left( {\frac{{{\mu _2}}}{{{\mu _1}}}} \right){v_2} \Rightarrow {v_1} = \sqrt 3 \,{v_2}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question: A ray of light travels from a medium of refractive index \[\mu \] to air. Its angle of incidence in the medium is \[\theta \], measured from the normal to the boundary, and its angle of deviation is \[\delta \] . \[\delta \] is plotted against \[\theta \]. Which of the following best represents the resulting curve?
explanation:
The refracted light ray is shown in the figure. https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764949931/preptracker/questions/q_32280_explanation_1764949931057.png The deviation of the light ray is
$\delta = r - \theta $
From Snell’s law $n\sin \theta = \sin r$
$\delta = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {n\sin \theta } \right) - \theta $
The graph between $\delta $ and $\theta $ is non linear but at
$\theta = {\theta _c} \Rightarrow r = {90^ \circ }$
$\delta = {90^ \circ } - {\theta _c}$
If $\theta > {\theta _c}$ then the ray undergoes TIR as shown in the figure. https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764949932/preptracker/questions/q_32280_explanation_1764949931891.png $\delta = 180 - 2\theta $
So just after crossing the deviation becomes double from to . ${90^ \circ } - {\theta _c}$ to $180 - 2{\theta _c}$.
The correct graph is (1).
subject: Physics
subtopic: Miscellaneous Problems on Refraction at Plane Surface

question: You are given four sources of light each one providing a light of a single colour-red, blue, green and yellow. Suppose the angle of refraction for a beam of yellow light corresponding to a particular angle of incidence at the interface of two media is ${\rm{90}}^\circ $.  Which of the following statements is correct if the source of yellow light replaced with that of other lights without changing the angle of incidence?
explanation: Among all given sources of light, the blue light have the smallest wavelength. According to Cauchy relationship, smaller the wavelength higher the refractive index and consequently smaller the critial angle. Critical angle for blue color is least which facilitates total internal reflection for the beam of blue light. The beam of green light would also undergo total internal reflection.

options.text: The beam of red light would bend towards normal while it gets refracted  through the second medium.
The beam of red light would undergo total internal reflection.
The beam of green light would bend away from the normal as it gets refracted  through the second medium.
The beam of blue  light would undergo total internal reflection.

subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question:
Diamonds are known for their spectacular brilliance, but diamonds found in nature rarely exhibit the brilliance.
By cutting the diamond suitably, multiple total internal reflections can be made to occur.
explanation: The critical angle for diamond - air interface is very small, therefore once light enters a diamond, it will undergo TIR. It is the skill of a diamond cutter which makes diamond to sparkle so brilliantly.

options.text: Statement A is correct but Statement B is incorrect.
Statement A is incorrect but Statement B is correct.

subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question:
Propagation of light through an optical fibre is due to total internal reflection taking place at the core-clade interface.
Refractive index of the material of the core of the optical fibre is greater than that of air.
explanation: Optical fibre communication is based on the phenomenon of total internal reflection at core-clad interface. The refractive index of the material of the cladding is more than that of air.

options.text: Statement A is correct but Statement B is incorrect.
Statement A is incorrect but Statement B is correct.

subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question:
Assertion: The air bubble shines in water.

Reason: Air bubble in water shines due to TIR of light.
explanation: Air bubble shines because of TIR. So option (1) is correct.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true and Reason is correct explanation of the Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are true but Reason is not the correct explanation of  the Assertion.
Assertion is true but Reason is false.
Assertion is false but Reason is true.

subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: In total internal reflection when the angle of incidence is equal to the critical angle for the pair of media in contact, what will be angle of refraction?
explanation: https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764955541/preptracker/questions/q_32157_explanation_1764955541212.png At , $i = {\theta _c}$ refracted ray grazes with the surface . So angle of refraction is 90°.
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: A light ray falls on a glass surface of refractive index $\sqrt 3 $ , at an angle 60°. The angle between the refracted and reflected rays would be
explanation:
https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764955532/preptracker/questions/q_32133_explanation_1764955531710.png Method (i)
By Snell’s law
$1\sin 60^\circ = \sqrt 3 \sin r$
$\frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{2} = \sqrt 3 \sin r$
$\sin r = \frac{1}{2}$$ \Rightarrow \,\,r = 30^\circ $
$\angle NQN' = 60^\circ + \angle RQS + 30^\circ $
$ \Rightarrow {180^ \circ } = {90^ \circ } + \angle RQS$
$ \Rightarrow $ Angle between refracted and reflected ray
$ = \angle RQS = {90^ \circ }$
Method (ii) Because angle of incidence is Brewster’s angle so that angle between reflected and refracted ray is 90°
$\tan {i_p} = \mu = \sqrt 3 $ (where subscript $P$ is for polarisation)
${i_p} = 60^\circ = i$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question: Wavelength of given light waves in air and in  a medium are $6000\,\mathop A\limits^ \circ  $ and  $4000\,\mathop A\limits^ \circ  $ respectively. The critical angle is
explanation:
Here ${\lambda _{air}} = 6000\mathop A\limits^ \circ ,\,\,{\lambda _{medium}} = 4000\mathop A\limits^ \circ $
Let $C$ be the critical angle
$\therefore \sin C = \frac{{{\mu _{air}}}}{{{\mu _{medium}}}} = \frac{{{\lambda _{medium}}}}{{{\lambda _{air}}}} = \left( {\frac{{4000}}{{6000}}} \right)$
$ \Rightarrow \sin C = \frac{2}{3} \Rightarrow C = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{2}{3}} \right)$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: The speed of light in media ${M_1}$ and ${M_2}$ are $1.5 \times {10^8}m{s^{ - 1}}$and $2 \times {10^8}m{s^{ - 1}}$ respectively.  A ray travels from medium ${M_1}$ to the medium ${M_2}$ with an angle of incidence $\theta $ . The ray suffers total internal reflection. Then  the value of the angle of incidence $\theta $  is
explanation:
Given,${v_1} = 1.5 \times {10^8}m{s^{ - 1}}$
${v_2} = 2.0 \times {10^8}m{s^{ - 1}}$
Refractive index for medium ${M_1}$ is
${\mu _1} = \frac{c}{{{v_1}}} = \frac{{3 \times {{10}^8}m{s^{ - 1}}}}{{1.5 \times {{10}^8}m{s^{ - 1}}}} = 2$
Refractive index for medium ${M_2}$ is
${\mu _2} = \frac{c}{{{v_2}}} = \frac{{3 \times {{10}^8}m{s^{ - 1}}}}{{2 \times {{10}^8}m{s^{ - 1}}}} = \frac{3}{2}$
When $\theta $ is the angle of incidence and $C$ is critical angle, for total internal reflection $\sin \theta > \sin C$
But, $\sin C = \frac{{{\mu _2}}}{{{\mu _1}}}$
$\therefore \,\,\,\,\,\,\sin \theta > \frac{{{\mu _2}}}{{{\mu _1}}} = \frac{{3{\rm{/}}2}}{2} \Rightarrow \theta > {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{3}{4}} \right)$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question:
A ray of light passes through four transparent media with refractive indices ${n_1},{n_2},{n_3}$ and ${n_4}$ as shown.
The surface of all media are parallel. If the emergent ray $DE$ is parallel to incident ray $AB$ then
explanation:
https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764949913/preptracker/questions/q_32102_explanation_1764949912048.png Apply Snell’s law
${n_1}\sin {\mkern 1mu} (i) = {n_2}\sin {\mkern 1mu} ({r_1})]\,\,\,\,\,\,(1)$
${n_2}\sin {\mkern 1mu} ({r_1}) = {n_3}\sin {\mkern 1mu} ({r_2})\,\,\,\,\,\,(2)$
${n_3}\sin {\mkern 1mu} ({r_2}) = {n_4}\sin {\mkern 1mu} (i)\,\,\,\,\,\,\,(3)$
From these three equations,
${n_1}\sin \,(i) = {n_4}\sin \,(i)$
$ \Rightarrow {n_1} = {n_4}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question: A transparent cube of $0.21\,m$ edge contains a small air bubble. Its apparent distance when viewed through one face of the cube is $0.10\,m$ and when viewed from the opposite face is $0.04\,m$. The actual distance of the bubble from the second face of the cube
explanation:
https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764949911/preptracker/questions/q_32098_explanation_1764949910637.png $\mu = \frac{{real\,\,{\rm{distance}}}}{{apparent\,\,{\rm{distance}}}} = \frac{{\left( {0.21 - x} \right)}}{{0.10}} = \frac{x}{{0.04}}$
$ \Rightarrow 0.21 \times 0.04 - x \times 0.04 = 0.10x$
$ \Rightarrow x = \frac{{0.21 \times 0.04}}{{0.14}} = x = 0.06\,m$
subject: Physics

question: A monochromatic beam of light is travelling from medium $A$ of refractive index ${n_1}$ to a medium $B$ of refractive index ${n_2}$ . In the medium $A$, there are $x$ number of waves in certain distance. In the medium $B$, there are $y$ number of waves in the same distance. Then, refractive index of medium $A$ with respect to medium $B$ is ......
explanation:
If there are $x$ and $y$ number of waves in length $s$ in mediums $A$ and $B$ respectively, wavelengths in these mediums are, ${\lambda _A} = \frac{s}{x}$ and ${\lambda _B} = \frac{s}{y}$ respectively.
Refractive index of medium $A$ with respect to $B$ is
$\frac{{{n_a}}}{{{n_b}}} = \frac{{{\lambda _B}}}{{{\lambda _A}}} = \frac{{s{\rm{/}}y}}{{s{\rm{/}}x}} = \frac{x}{y}\,$
subject: Physics

question: A ray of light enters from a rarer to a denser medium. The angle of incidence is $i$. Then the reflected and refracted rays are mutually perpendicular to each other. The critical angle for the pair of media is
explanation:
https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764949907/preptracker/questions/q_32082_explanation_1764949907510.png From figure (a),
$90^\circ - i + 90^\circ - r = 90^\circ $
$\therefore \,\,\,\,\,i + r = {90^o}$
$ \Rightarrow \mu = \frac{{\sin i}}{{\sin r}} = \frac{{\sin i}}{{\sin \left( {{{90}^o} - i} \right)}} = \tan i$
From figure (b),
$\frac{{\sin {{90}^o}}}{{\sin C}} = \mu \Rightarrow \sin C = \frac{1}{\mu } = \cot i$
$\therefore $ Critical angle C = ${\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\cot i} \right)$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: If ${\varepsilon _0}$ and ${\mu _0}$ are the permittivity and permeability of free space and $\varepsilon $ and  $\mu $ are the 	corresponding quantities for a medium, then refractive index of the medium is
explanation:
Refractive index of the medium is
$n = \frac{{c\left( {speed\,of\,light\,in\,vaccum} \right)}}{{v\left( {speed\,of\,light\,in\,medium} \right)}}$
But $c = \frac{1}{{\sqrt {{\mu _0}{\varepsilon _0}} }}$ and $v = \frac{1}{{\sqrt {\mu \varepsilon } }}$
$\therefore n = \frac{{\frac{1}{{\sqrt {{\mu _0}{\varepsilon _0}} }}}}{{\frac{1}{{\sqrt {\mu \varepsilon } }}}} = \sqrt {\frac{{\mu \varepsilon }}{{{\mu _0}{\varepsilon _0}}}} $
subject: Physics

question: The time required for the light to pass through a glass slab (refractive index = 1.5) of thickness $4\,mm$ is ( $c = 3 \times {10^8}m{s^{ - 1}}$, speed of light in free space)
explanation:
Speed of light in glass slab is
$v = \frac{c}{\mu } = \frac{{3 \times {{10}^8}}}{{1.5}}m{s^{ - 1}}\,\,\,\,\,\,(1)$
The time required for the light to pass through a glass slab of thickness $4\,mm$ is
$t = \frac{{{\rm{thickness}}}}{{{\rm{velocity}}\left( v \right)}} = \frac{{4 \times {{10}^{ - 3}}m}}{{\frac{{3 \times {{10}^8}m{s^{ - 1}}}}{{1.5}}}}\,\,\,[{\rm{using}}\,(1)]$
$ = 2 \times {10^{ - 11}}{\rm{s}}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Miscellaneous Problems on Refraction at Plane Surface

question: White light is incident normally on a glass slab. Inside the glass slab,
explanation: White light is incident normally on a glass slab. Inside the glass slab  red light travels faster than other colours as its wavelength is highest.

options.text: Red light travels faster than other colours
Violet light travels faster than other colours
Yellow light travels faster than other colours
All colours travel with the same speed

subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question:
A boat has green light of wavelength $\lambda = 500\,nm$ on the mast. What wavelength would be measured and what colour would be observed for this light as seen by a driver submerged in water by the side of the boat?
Given ${n_w} = \frac{4}{3}$
explanation:
Here, ${n_w} = \frac{4}{3},{\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\lambda _a} = 500nm$
As ${n_w} = \frac{{{\lambda _a}}}{{{\lambda _w}}}$,
[subscripts $a$ and $w$ for air and water respectively]
$\therefore {\lambda _w} = \frac{{500\,nm}}{{\left( {4/3} \right)}} = 375\,nm$
Colour of light is determined by its frequency and not by its wavelength. As frequency does not change on refraction, colour will not change and will remain green.
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: A ray of light is travelling from glass to air. (Refractive index of glass = 1.5). The angle of incidence is ${50^o}$. The deviation of the ray is
explanation:
https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764955515/preptracker/questions/q_31995_explanation_1764955515087.png Given, $^a{\mu _g} = 1.5.$
Critical angle for glass $ = {\sin ^{ - 1}}(1/1.5) \approx 42^\circ $
Hence a ray of light incident at ${50^o}$ in glass undergoes total internal reflection.
$\therefore $ Total deviation of the ray
$\delta = 180^\circ - \left( {50^\circ + 50^\circ } \right) = 80^\circ $
subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question: $A,{\rm{ }}B$ and $C$ are the parallel sided transparent media of refractive index ${n_1},{n_2}$ and ${n_3}$ respectively. They are arranged as shown in the figure. A ray is incident at an angle $\theta $ on the surface of separation of $A$ and $B$ which is as shown in the figure. After the refraction into the medium $B$, the ray grazes the surface of separation of the media $B$ and $C$. Then sin $\theta $ =
explanation:
https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764949899/preptracker/questions/q_31991_explanation_1764949898884.png Applying Snell’s law at M
${n_1}\sin \theta = {n_2}\sin \theta '\,\,\,\,\,\,(1)$
Again, applying Snell’s law at N
${n_2}\sin \theta ' = {n_3}\sin {90^o} = {n_3}$
$\therefore \,{n_1}\sin \theta = {n_3}\,\,\,\,\,\,[{\rm{Using}}\,(1)]$
$ \Rightarrow $ $\sin \theta = \frac{{{n_3}}}{{{n_1}}}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question:
A ray of light travelling through glass of refractive index $\sqrt 2 $ is incident on glass - air boundary at an angle of incidence $45^\circ $ . If refractive index of air is 1, then the angle of refraction will be
$\left[ {\sin {{45}^o} = \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }},\sin {{90}^o} = 1} \right]$
explanation:
from snell’s law
${\mu _i}\sin i = {\mu _r}\sin r$
Where, refractive index of glass is ${\mu _i}$, angle of incidence is $i$, refractive index of air is ${\mu _r}$ and angle of refraction is $r$
Here, ${\mu _i} = \sqrt 2 ,i = {45^o},{\mu _r} = 1$
$ \Rightarrow \left( {\sqrt 2 } \right)\sin {45^o} = \left( 1 \right)\sin r$
$\left( {\sqrt 2 } \right)\left( {\frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}} \right) = \sin r$
$ \Rightarrow \sin r = 1 = \sin {90^o}$
$r = {90^o}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question: Glass has refractive index $\mu $ with respect to air and the critical angle for a ray of light going from glass to air is $\theta $ . If a ray of light is incident from air on the glass with angle of incidence , corresponding angle of refraction is
explanation:
Refractive index of glass with respect to air = $\mu $
Critical angle = $\theta $
$\therefore \mu = \frac{1}{{\sin \theta }}$ (1)
When a ray of light incident from air on the glass with angle of incidence $\theta $ . If $r$ be the angle of refraction, then by Snell’s law
$\mu = \frac{{\sin \theta }}{{\sin r}}$
$ \Rightarrow \sin r = \frac{{\sin \theta }}{\mu } = \frac{{1{\rm{/}}\mu }}{\mu }\,\,\,\,\,\,\,[{\rm{from}}\,{\rm{eq}}.(1)]$
$ \Rightarrow \sin r = \frac{1}{{{\mu ^2}}}$
$r = {\sin ^{ - 1}}\left( {\frac{1}{{{\mu ^2}}}} \right)$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Refraction of Light at Plane Surface

question: A plane glass plate is placed over a various coloured letters( violet, green, yellow, red). The letter which appears to raised more is
explanation:
Shift in the image of letter due to glass plate,
$s = \left( {d - \frac{1}{\mu }} \right) = d\left( {1 - \frac{1}{\mu }} \right)$
Since, ${\lambda _v} < {\lambda _g} < {\lambda _y} < {\lambda _r};$
$\therefore \,\,\,{\mu _v} > {\mu _g} > {\mu _y} > {\mu _r}.$
$Hence,{\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {s_v} > {s_g} > {s_y} > {s_r}\,;$
i.e., maximum shift is observed for violet letters.
subject: Physics

question: A fish in water (refractive index $n$) looks at a bird vertically above in the air. If $y$ is the height of the bird and  $x{\rm{ }}$ is the depth of the fish from the surface, then the distance of the bird as estimated by the fish is
explanation:
https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764955513/preptracker/questions/q_31975_explanation_1764955513360.png The apparent distance of the surface of water for the fish $ = x$
Also, from the surface, the bird is at an apparent distance $ny$.
$\therefore $The total apparent distance $ = x + ny$
subject: Physics

question: A vessel of height $2d$ is half filled with a liquid of refractive index $\sqrt 2 $ and the other half with a liquid of refractive index $n$.(The given liquids are immiscible). Then the apparent depth of the  inner surface of the bottom of the vessel (neglecting the thickness of the bottom of the vessel) will be
explanation:
$\mu = \frac{{real\,depth}}{{apparent\,depth}}$
$\therefore $Due to ${1^{st}}$ liquid, $\sqrt 2 = \frac{d}{{{x_1}}}$ $ \Rightarrow {x_1} = \frac{d}{{\sqrt 2 }}$
Due to the second liquid, $n = \frac{d}{{{x_2}}}$
$\therefore \,\,\,\,\,{x_2} = \frac{d}{n}$
$\therefore $ Total apparent depth $ = {x_1} + {x_2} = \frac{d}{{\sqrt 2 }} + \frac{d}{n}$
$ = \frac{{d\left( {n + \sqrt 2 } \right)}}{{n\sqrt 2 }}$
subject: Physics

question: A point source of light is kept below the surface of water $\left( {{n_w} = \frac{4}{3}} \right)$ at a depth of $\sqrt 7 m$ . The radius of the circular bright patch of light noticed on the surface of water is ........ $m$
explanation:
Here, Refractive index of water, ${n_w} = \frac{4}{3}$
$\therefore \,\,\sin C = \frac{1}{{{n_w}}} = \frac{1}{{\left( {\frac{4}{3}} \right)}} = \frac{3}{4}\,\,\,\,[C = critical\,\,angle]$
$ \Rightarrow \tan C = \frac{3}{{\sqrt {{4^2} - {3^2}} }} = \frac{3}{{\sqrt 7 }}\,\,\,\,\,\,(1)$
From figure, https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764949897/preptracker/questions/q_31967_explanation_1764949897355.png $\tan C = \frac{R}{{\sqrt 7 }}\,\,\,\,\,\,(2)$
Equating (1) and (2) , we get
$\frac{3}{{\sqrt 7 }} = \frac{R}{{\sqrt 7 }} \Rightarrow R = 3\,{\mkern 1mu} m$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Critical angle and Total Internal Reflection

question: If $_j{{\bf{\mu }}_i}$ represents refractive index  medium $i$ w.r.to medium $j$.  Then the product $_2{\mu _1} \times {\,_3}{\mu _2} \times {\,_4}{\mu _3}$  is equal to
subject: Physics

options.text: Passing of light from one medium to another
